items.title: Zimbabwe to host Bangladesh for a full series
items.summary: The two teams will play a Test match, three ODIs and three T20Is in Harare and Bulawayo

items.title: This team can go much further: Shanto aspires for more after Pakistan success
items.summary: In an exclusive conversation with Cricbuzz, the Bangladesh Test skipper opens up on the team's historic series win over Pakistan, his own form and a shift in mindset

items.title: Bangladesh to tour Zimbabwe for all-format series in June-July
items.summary: Upcoming series will mark Zimbabwe's first home assignment this year, and their first international action since the T20 World Cup

items.title: Shanto praises 'healthy competition' within bowling unit after series win
items.summary: Bangladesh captain highlighted the bowlers' consistency and competitiveness as central to the series victory against Pakistan

items.title: Masood leaves captaincy future to PCB but calls for 'structural' changes
items.summary: After a series loss to Bangladesh, Pakistan captain urged his side to reflect on recurring failures and address the "root causes" behind their slide in Tests

items.title: Taijul takes six as Bangladesh complete 2-0 sweep
items.summary: Rizwan's 94 went in vain as Pakistan fell 78 short of their target of 437

items.title: 'We'll bowl Pakistan out,' says Bangladesh bowling coach Shaun Tait
items.summary: Bangladesh's bowling coach also praises Litton Das' wicketkeeping for helping break Pakistan's strong partnership

items.title: Tempers flare between Litton and Rizwan in Sylhet
items.summary: Bangladesh's fielders got stuck into the Pakistan batter during the chase on the fourth day in Sylhet

items.title: Batting coach Asad Shafiq hopeful of improbable Pakistan victory
items.summary: Chasing 437 to level the series in Sylhet, Pakistan need 121 runs to win with only three wickets in hand

items.title: Rizwan and Agha resist but Bangladesh on course for 2-0 sweep
items.summary: Going into the fifth day in Sylhet, the hosts need three wickets to wrap up victory

items.title: Mushfiqur ton, Litton and Joy fifties set Pakistan 437
items.summary: Shahzad picked up four wickets but Mushfiqur ensured Bangladesh remained well in front

items.title: BCB elections to elect new president to be held on June 7
items.summary: Former Bangladesh captain Tamim Iqbal is the current BCB president

items.title: Taskin, Mehidy, Taijul, Mahmudul put Bangladesh well in front
items.summary: Taijul Islam and Nahid Ranatook three wickets each after Taskin and Mehidy shared the first four to restrict Pakistan to 232
